medications.indication_rationale: Reduce surface bacterial load across affected areas.
Apply to the most inflamed areas.
medications.dose_route_frequency: apply twice daily to worst areas (as per source note)
10 mg orally daily (as per source note)

medications.indication_rationale: Not indicated for benign superficial folliculitis without deep/systemic features.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Wear loose breathable cotton, shower immediately after exercise and change out of damp gear, wash athletic gear in hot water, and avoid shaving the area until healed.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Removes the friction/occlusion/sweat trigger and prevents recurrence/spread.

patient_education_points.point: Tight sweaty clothing irritated your hair follicles and let normal skin bacteria cause hundreds of tiny surface infections — it looks dramatic but is superficial and settles quickly.
Use the antibacterial wash, the ointment on the worst spots, and loose clothing. Don't shave the area until it heals.
If the spots merge into large deep painful hot lumps, or you get a fever/chills or spreading deep redness — seek review promptly.

follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Deep painful lumps, spreading redness, or fever/chills — seek prompt/urgent review.

must_include_items: Diagnose benign superficial folliculitis and exclude hot-tub aetiology and deeper/systemic infection
Antibacterial wash + topical antibacterial + itch relief
Trigger control (loose clothing, wash after exercise, avoid shaving)
Safety-net for furuncle/abscess or systemic infection
errors_of_commission: Systemic antibiotics/ED referral for benign superficial folliculitis
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires correct superficial-folliculitis management with trigger control and a safety-net for deeper/systemic infection; avoid over-treatment.
